own_review.issue: main() only checks args.length < 1 but unconditionally reads args[1], args[2] and Arrays.copyOfRange(args, 3, ...); invoking with 1-2 args throws ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException/IllegalArgumentException instead of printing usage

Shared config now stores ALL properties except fluo.connection.* into Zookeeper, which includes fluo.accumulo.password/fluo.accumulo.user and any deprecated fluo.client.* props (they do not match CONNECTION_PREFIX). Plaintext Accumulo credentials land in ZK shared config; at base only fluo.app.* and tx rollback time were shared
Stray statement `Arrays.copyOfRange(args, 3, args.length);` whose result is discarded (dead code)
main() catches Exception, logs it, and falls out of main with exit code 0; startup failures are invisible to wrapper scripts/process supervisors
Unused imports (URI, FileUtils, Hadoop Configuration/FileSystem/Path, SimpleConfiguration) and unused `log` field; same unused-import problem in FluoGetProp.java (incl. unused slf4j imports)
FluoScan.ScanOptions is a verbatim duplicate of the new AppRunner.ScanOptions inner class; scan option parsing now exists in multiple copies
Recursively deletes the user-supplied downloadPath directory (FileUtils.deleteDirectory) before copying jars; a mistyped path silently destroys data
Help flag exits with status 1 in FluoInit but 0 in FluoScan; inconsistent exit codes

--update path is only reachable after the oracleExists() abort, so shared configuration can no longer be updated while an application is running (behavior regression vs intent of updateSharedConfig javadoc)

points.point: docs/applications.md example incomplete, 'will fail at runtime' because hasRequiredClientProps needs user/password/instance
points.note: Contradicted by the PR itself: the updated FluoFactory.newClient javadoc (visible in diff) states the only required property is fluo.connection.application.name; the new design fetches remaining config from Zookeeper. Claim was verified only against base-repo FluoClientImpl

points.point: No public API for multi-file loading; load(InputStream) is private
points.note: False: the PR makes load(InputStream) and load(File) public in SimpleConfiguration (visible in diff) precisely to enable layered loading

points.point: FluoConfiguration constructors lack multi-file support, limiting split-config to 'choose one file'
points.note: False: tests in the diff layer configs via new FluoConfiguration(connFile); config.load(appFile)

points.point: docs/metrics.md reference to fluo.properties not updated
points.note: False: the PR updates exactly those metrics.md references to fluo-app.properties (visible in diff)

points.point: OracleRunnable/WorkerRunnable hardcode conf/fluo.properties; functional concern if PR does not address it
points.note: Hedged, base-verified speculation about classes the PR explicitly deprecates; old YARN path intentionally keeps the legacy single-file format

points.point: FluoInstall.getFluoPropsPath still returns fluo.properties and needs updating
points.note: FluoInstall is @Deprecated in this PR; leaving the legacy path on the old file is intentional

missed: human: FluoExec should pass old-style config through and have new config only set app name
Accumulo credentials/deprecated fluo.client.* props stored in ZK shared config
Unused imports/log in FluoConfig and FluoGetProp
FluoGetJars recursive delete of user-supplied path

points.point: FluoConfigurationTest:236 asserts applicationProps.exists() instead of just-declared connectionProps
points.note: Verified in diff: copy-paste bug, real
False: the PR changes that javadoc to CONNECTION_ZOOKEEPERS_PROP (visible in diff)

points.point: AppRunner.scan() return type changed long -> void, breaking API change
points.note: Verified; class is deprecated but observation is accurate

points.point: SimpleConfiguration load() private -> public API expansion
points.note: Verified; minor quibble that PR description does mention multi-file loading

points.point: hasRequiredAdminProps check moved from FluoAdminImpl constructor into initialize(), delaying failure

points.note: True but cosmetic; @Deprecated(since=) suggestion is anachronistic for this Java version

points.point: getApplicationName() now throws NoSuchElementException instead of IllegalArgumentException when unset
points.note: Verified: base threw IAE ('Application name cannot be null') via verifyApplicationName(null); new code throws NoSuchElementException. Attribution to getNonEmptyString is slightly off but the substance is correct

points.point: hasRequiredProps dual-prefix checks need an explanatory comment
FluoList static FluoAdminImpl.oracleExists - verify static conversion
points.note: Properly hedged; definition is in the truncated portion

points.point: NOTE listing methods defined beyond truncation
points.note: Honest handling of truncation, not a finding

missed: human: FluoExec should pass old-style config through and have new config only set app name
Accumulo credentials/deprecated fluo.client.* props stored in ZK shared config
Unused imports/log in FluoConfig and FluoGetProp
FluoGetJars recursive delete of user-supplied path
exceeds: FluoConfigurationTest copy-paste assertion bug (missed by human and by my review)
getApplicationName exception-type change IAE -> NoSuchElementException (missed by human and by my review)

qualitative: v4 is dominated by absence-fabrications: four of its six points assert that the PR fails to do things the full diff visibly does (public load() methods for multi-file layering, updated metrics.md references, a doc example consistent with the new connection-only client design), all verified only against the 7k-truncated diff or the base repo. v5, working from the fuller diff, produced mostly real, verifiable findings, including a genuine test copy-paste bug and an exception-type behavior change that both the human reviewer and my own review missed, and it handled the remaining truncation honestly by hedging rather than fabricating; its one fabrication is claiming a javadoc was left stale when the PR updates it. Both candidates missed the more substantive issues: the FluoInit argument-handling crash, credentials being written into Zookeeper shared config by the new updateSharedConfig, and the human's FluoExec config pass-through design point. Net result: v5 (-2) clearly outperforms v4 (-12), with v5 exceeding the reference on two verified findings.
